---
title: "Source Selection Map"
author: "Anoob Prakash"
date: '2023-05-10'
output:
  rmarkdown::html_document:
    theme: cosmo  
    number_sections: false
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{css zoom-lib-src, echo = FALSE}
script src = "https://ajax.googleapis.com/ajax/libs/jquery/3.4.1/jquery.min.js"
```

```{js zoom-jquery, echo = FALSE}
 $(document).ready(function() {
    $('body').prepend('<div class=\"zoomDiv\"><img src=\"\" class=\"zoomImg\"></div>');
    // onClick function for all plots (img's)
    $('img:not(.zoomImg)').click(function() {
      $('.zoomImg').attr('src', $(this).attr('src')).css({width: '100%'});
      $('.zoomDiv').css({opacity: '1', width: 'auto', border: '1px solid white', borderRadius: '5px', position: 'fixed', top: '50%', left: '50%', marginRight: '-50%', transform: 'translate(-50%, -50%)', boxShadow: '0px 0px 50px #888888', zIndex: '50', overflow: 'auto', maxHeight: '100%'});
    });
    // onClick function for zoomImg
    $('img.zoomImg').click(function() {
      $('.zoomDiv').css({opacity: '0', width: '0%'}); 
    });
  });
```

## Files  
```{r}
# packages
require(terra)
require(tigris)
require(elevatr)
require(raster)
require(tidyterra)
require(ggplot2)
require(sf)
require(ggthemes)
require(rnaturalearth)
require(scatterpie)
require(viridis)
require(sf)
require(rnaturalearth)
require(viridis)
require(tidyverse)
require(ggforce)
```


```{r}
# set working directory 
# setwd("~/mydata/Anoob/TNC/ANALYSIS") # wright
# setwd("/netfiles02/kellrlab/Anoob/TNC/ANALYSIS") # VACC
setwd("Z:/Anoob/Polygenic_Adaptation/ANALYSIS") # local
# setwd("~/mydata/Anoob/TNC/ANALYSIS") # wright
```


## Data  
```{r}
# incorporate ancestry score
# Get ID and pop info for each individual
pop<-read.table("filtering_the_snps_across_REGION/REGIONS/324RSfam_18BSfam_bam_VACC.list")
pop$Family <- str_replace_all(pop$V1,"/netfiles02/kellrlab/datashare/Spruce/exome_capture/WES_mapping/Mapping_WES/ref_Pmariana/BWA/Prubens/NSF/","")
pop$Family <- str_replace_all(pop$Family,"/netfiles02/kellrlab/datashare/Spruce/exome_capture/WES_mapping/Mapping_WES/ref_Pmariana/BWA/Pmariana/","")

pop$Family <- str_replace_all(pop$Family,".sorted.rmdup.bam","")
pop <- pop$Family
dataset <- read.table("filtering_the_snps_across_REGION/admix/Full_Sampling_intersect_poly_updated_with_BS_samples_PCA_admix_K2.admix.2.Q")
colnames(dataset) <- c("q1","q2")

admix.id = as.data.frame(cbind(pop, dataset))
names(admix.id) <- c("Family","q1","q2")

# Meta data
Meta <- read.table("../../BlackSpruce/Info_samples_all.txt", sep="\t",header=T)

# add coordinates 
dataset <- merge(admix.id,Meta[,c("Family","Longitude","Latitude")])

```

## Map assests

```{r}
# Get map data
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")
map_area <- world %>% filter(continent == "North America") %>%
  st_crop(xmin = -95, xmax = -59, ymin = 32, ymax = 49)

plot(map_area)
bbox = st_bbox(map_area)

# spruce range
little_range<-st_read('../../../datashare/Spruce/SpatialData/rangemaps/spruce/picerube/picerube.shp')
bs_range<-st_read('../../../datashare/Spruce/SpatialData/rangemaps/spruce/picemariana/picemari.shp')

# state boundary
# states <- st_as_sf(map("state", plot = FALSE, fill = TRUE)) # old

# Get US states
us_states <- ne_states(country = "united states of america", returnclass = "sf")

# Get Canadian provinces and territories
canada_provinces <- ne_states(country = "canada", returnclass = "sf")

# Combine US states and Canadian provinces
states <- rbind(us_states, canada_provinces)


# world_sf <- st_as_sf(world)

lakes <- ne_download(scale = 10, type = "lakes", category = "physical", 
                     returnclass = "sf")

# setting the projection
# world_sf <- st_as_sf(world, coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326)
lakes_transformed <- st_transform(lakes, st_crs(map_area))
little_range_sf <- st_transform(little_range, st_crs(map_area))
bs_range_sf <- st_transform(bs_range, st_crs(map_area))
states_sf <- st_transform(states, st_crs(map_area))



# Ensure all data is in the same coordinate system
print(st_crs(map_area))
target_crs <- st_crs(4326)

map_area <- st_transform(map_area, target_crs)
bs_range_sf <- st_transform(bs_range_sf, target_crs)
little_range_sf <- st_transform(little_range_sf, target_crs)
states <- st_transform(states, target_crs)
lakes_transformed <- st_transform(lakes_transformed, target_crs)

print(st_crs(bs_range_sf))
print(st_crs(little_range_sf))
print(st_crs(states_sf))
print(st_crs(lakes_transformed))
```

```{r}
# Determine appropriate UTM zone based on your data's location
utm_zone <- floor((mean(st_coordinates(map_area)[,1]) + 180)/6) + 1
utm_crs <- st_crs(paste0("+proj=utm +zone=", utm_zone, " +datum=WGS84"))

map_area_projected <- st_transform(map_area, utm_crs)
bs_range_projected <- st_transform(bs_range_sf, utm_crs)
little_range_projected <- st_transform(little_range_sf, utm_crs)
states_projected <- st_transform(states_sf, utm_crs)
lakes_projected <- st_transform(lakes_transformed, utm_crs)

# snap geometries together
tolerance <- 1e-9  # Adjust as needed (in meters for UTM)
bs_range_snapped <- st_snap(bs_range_projected, map_area_projected, tolerance)
lakes_snapped <- st_snap(lakes_projected, map_area_projected, tolerance)
little_range_snapped <- st_snap(little_range_projected, map_area_projected, tolerance)
states_snapped <- st_snap(states_projected, map_area_projected, tolerance)



# transform back to WGS84
bs_range_sf <- st_transform(bs_range_snapped, 4326)
little_range_sf <- st_transform(little_range_snapped, 4326)
states_sf <- st_transform(states_snapped, 4326)
lakes_transformed <- st_transform(lakes_snapped, 4326)

# # ensure the geometries are valid 
bs_range_sf <- st_make_valid(bs_range_sf)
little_range_sf <- st_make_valid(little_range_sf)
states_sf <- st_make_valid(states_sf)
lakes_transformed <- st_make_valid(lakes_transformed)

```

### Elevation data   

- Run once 
```{r echo = T, eval = F}
# Get elevation data
# elevation <- get_elev_raster(world %>% filter(continent == "North America"), z = 7)

# Use elevatr to get elevation data
elev <- elevatr::get_elev_raster(
  locations = map_area, z = 10) # , clip = 'locations'

# saveRDS(elev,"./elevatr/elev_north_america_zoom_10")

# convert to terra and mask area of interest
mdt <- rast(elev) %>% 
         mask(vect(map_area))

# load this for future analysis
# saveRDS(mdt,"./elevatr/elev_north_america_masked_zoom_10_2")
plot(mdt)
```

```{r}
# load saved data 
elev <- readRDS("./elevatr/elev_north_america_zoom_10")

mdt <- readRDS("./elevatr/elev_north_america_masked_zoom_10_2")

plot(mdt)
```

### Test different palettes  
```{r}
# Hypso gradient
grad_hypso <- hypso.colors2(10, "dem_poster")


# Try some options, but we need to be aware of the values of our raster

r_limits <- minmax(mdt) %>% as.vector()

# Rounded to lower and upper 500
r_limits <- c(floor(r_limits[1] / 200), ceiling(r_limits[2] / 200)) * 200

# And making min value to 0.
r_limits <- pmax(r_limits, 0)

# testing
elevt_test <- ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(data = mdt)

# Create a helper function

plot_pal_test <- function(pal) {
  elevt_test +
    scale_fill_hypso_tint_c(
      limits = r_limits,
      palette = pal
    ) +
    ggtitle(pal) +
    theme_minimal()
}

plot_pal_test("etopo1_hypso")
plot_pal_test("dem_poster")
plot_pal_test("spain") # chosen 
plot_pal_test("pakistan")
plot_pal_test("utah_1")
plot_pal_test("wiki-2.0_hypso")
plot_pal_test("dem_screen")
```

### Themeing 
```{r}
# load font
myload_fonts <- function(fontname, family,
                         fontdir = tempdir()) {
  fontname_url <- utils::URLencode(fontname)
  fontzip <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip")
  download.file(paste0("https://fonts.google.com/download?family=", fontname_url),
    fontzip,
    quiet = TRUE,
    mode = "wb"
  )
  unzip(fontzip,
    exdir = fontdir,
    junkpaths = TRUE
  )

  # Load fonts
  paths <- list(
    regular = "Regular.ttf",
    bold = "Bold.ttf",
    italic = "Italic.ttf",
    bolditalic = "BoldItalic.ttf"
  )


  namefile <- gsub(" ", "", fontname)
  paths_end <- file.path(
    fontdir,
    paste(namefile, paths, sep = "-")
  )


  names(paths_end) <- names(paths)

  sysfonts::font_add(family,
    regular = paths_end["regular"],
    bold = paths_end["bold"],
    italic = paths_end["italic"],
    bolditalic = paths_end["bolditalic"]
  )

  return(invisible())
}

# Theming
myload_fonts("Noto Serif", "notoserif", "~/R/googlefonts")
showtext::showtext_auto()

# Adjust text size
base_text_size <- 30
```


## Introgression plot - version 1.0 - for checking the data 

```{r}
# tidy up the ancestry data frame
dataset2 <- dataset[,c("Family","q1","q2","Longitude","Latitude")]
dataset2$Family <- as.factor(dataset2$Family)
dataset2$q1 <- round(dataset2$q1,3)
dataset2$q2 <- round(dataset2$q2,3)
dataset2$radius <- dataset2$q2
colnames(dataset2) <- c("Family","RedSpruce","BlackSpruce","Longitude","Latitude","Radius")

```


```{r}
## introgression map
int_map <- ggplot() +
  # Add bg
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = -90, xmax = -59, ymin = 34, ymax = 49), fill = "white", color = NA) +
  # base map
  geom_sf(data = map_area, fill = "grey90", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
  # Add state lines
  geom_sf(data = states_sf, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.6) +  
  # Add littles range for spruces
  geom_sf(data = bs_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "grey20", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_sf(data = little_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "#8E063B", alpha = 0.5) +
  # Add lakes
  geom_sf(data = lakes_transformed, fill = "white", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
  # Add ancestry proportions
  geom_scatterpie2(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, r = 0.25), 
                   data = dataset2 %>% filter(Radius < 0.75), 
                   cols = c("RedSpruce", "BlackSpruce"), color = "black",
                   alpha = 0.8,
                   legend_name = "Ancestry") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("RedSpruce" = "red", "BlackSpruce" = "black")) + 
  # Add allopatric BS site
  geom_point(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude), 
             data = dataset2 %>% filter(Radius > 0.75),
             shape = 23, size = 5, alpha = 0.9, 
             fill="orange",color="black") + 
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = -78.3, xmax = -74.7, ymin = 40, ymax = 42), fill = NA, color = "black", lwd = 1.4) +
  # map viewable area
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-95, -59), ylim = c(34, 48), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect("white", colour = NA),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.2),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.0),
        axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.4),
        legend.position = "none")
```


```{r}
dataset3 <- dataset2
dataset3$Population <- stringr::str_split_i(dataset3$Family,pattern = "_",1)

int_map_inset <- ggplot() +
  # Add bg
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = -90, xmax = -59, ymin = 34, ymax = 49), fill = "white", color = NA) +
  # base map
  geom_sf(data = map_area, fill = "grey90", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
  # Add state lines
  # geom_sf(data = states_sf, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.6) +  
  # Add littles range for spruces
  geom_sf(data = bs_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "grey20", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_sf(data = little_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "#8E063B", alpha = 0.5) +
  # Add lakes
  geom_sf(data = lakes_transformed, fill = "white", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
  # Add ancestry proportions
  geom_scatterpie2(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, r = 0.05), 
                   data = dataset2 %>% filter(Radius < 0.75), 
                   cols = c("RedSpruce", "BlackSpruce"),
                   alpha = 0.7, color = NA,
                   legend_name = "Ancestry") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("RedSpruce" = "red", "BlackSpruce" = "black")) + 
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-78.3, -74.7), ylim = c(40, 42), expand = FALSE) +
  geom_mark_ellipse(data = dataset3,
                    aes(color = Population,
                        label = Population,
                        x = Longitude, y = Latitude)) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect("white", colour = NA),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.2),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.0),
        axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.4),
        legend.position = "none")

# ggsave("./figures/paper_figures/Figure2A_inset.pdf", width = 3000, height = 2500, units = "px")

# Introgression supplementary figure - Pop level labelling
pop_int_supplementary <- ggplot() +
  # Add bg
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = -90, xmax = -59, ymin = 34, ymax = 49), fill = "white", color = NA) +
  # base map
  geom_sf(data = map_area, fill = "grey90", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
  # Add state lines
  # geom_sf(data = states_sf, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.6) +  
  # Add littles range for spruces
  geom_sf(data = bs_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "grey20", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_sf(data = little_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "#8E063B", alpha = 0.5) +
  # Add lakes
  geom_sf(data = lakes_transformed, fill = "white", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
  # Add ancestry proportions
  geom_scatterpie2(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, r = 0.20), 
                   data = dataset2 %>% filter(Radius < 0.75), 
                   cols = c("RedSpruce", "BlackSpruce"),
                   alpha = 0.7, color = NA,
                   legend_name = "Ancestry") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("RedSpruce" = "red", "BlackSpruce" = "black")) + 
  # Add allopatric BS site
  # geom_text_repel(data = dataset3, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, label = Population, group = Population)) +
  geom_mark_ellipse(data = dataset3,
                    aes(color = Population,
                        label = Population,
                        x = Longitude, y = Latitude)) +
  # map viewable area
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-95, -59), ylim = c(34, 48), expand = FALSE) +
  # coord_sf(xlim = c(-78.3, -74.7), ylim = c(40, 42), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect("white", colour = NA),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.2),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.0),
        axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.4),
        legend.position = "none")
```


## Introgression Map - Version 2.0
```{r}
require(conStruct) # conStruct package for admix proportions 
require(maps)
require(mapdata)
require(maptools)  #for shapefiles
require(scales)  #for transparency
require(plotrix)

# data long format 
pie_data <- tidyr::pivot_longer(dataset2,cols=RedSpruce:BlackSpruce, names_to = "Species",values_to="Ancestry")

admix.props <- pie_data$Ancestry

# make.structure.plot(admix.proportions = admix_props)

# Step 1: Reshape the data
admix_data <- pie_data %>%
  select(Family, Ancestry, Species) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Ancestry, values_fill = 0) %>%
  arrange(Family)

# Step 2: Create the admixture proportion matrix
admix_props <- as.matrix(admix_data[, c("RedSpruce", "BlackSpruce")])
rownames(admix_props) <- admix_data$Family

# Step 3: Create the coordinates matrix
coords <- pie_data %>%
  distinct(Family, Longitude, Latitude) %>%
  arrange(Family) %>%
  select(Longitude, Latitude) %>%
  as.matrix()

# Now you have:
# admix_props: a matrix of admixture proportions
# coords: a matrix of coordinates

make.structure.plot(admix.proportions = admix_props, sort.by = 1, layer.colors = c("red","black"))

make.admix.pie.plot(admix.proportions = admix_props, coords = coords, radii = 4, layer.colors = c("red","black"))

# maps::map(xlim = range(data.block$coords[,1]) + c(-5,5), ylim = range(data.block$coords[,2])+c(-2,2), col="gray")


## map    
# Define the map boundaries for eastern North America
xlim <- c(-95, -59)
ylim <- c(34, 48)

# read layers 
## BS range
black_range <- st_read("../../../datashare/Spruce/SpatialData/rangemaps/spruce/picemariana/picemari.shp") 
black_range_sf <- st_transform(black_range, crs = 4326)
# RS range
red_range <- st_read("../../../datashare/Spruce/SpatialData/rangemaps/spruce/picerube/picerube.shp") 
red_range_sf <- st_transform(red_range, crs = 4326)

library(sf)
library(scales)
```

### Admixture plot   
```{r echo=T, eval=F}
# Open PDF device
pdf("./figures/paper_figures/Figure2A_admixture_map_July_2025.pdf", 
    width = 19,   # width in inches
    height = 10,   # height in inches
    pointsize = 12)  # font size
```


```{r}
# Draw the base map
map("worldHires","usa", xlim = c(-95, -59), ylim = c(34, 48), col = "gray90", fill = TRUE, border = "gray50")
map("worldHires","Canada", xlim = c(-95, -59), ylim = c(34, 48), col = "gray90", fill = TRUE, border = "gray50",
    add=TRUE)
map("state", xlim = c(-95, -59), ylim = c(34, 48), col = "gray90", fill = TRUE, border = "gray50",
    add=TRUE)
# Add lakes
map("lakes", col = "white", fill = TRUE, add = TRUE)
# Add custom shape files 
## BS range
plot(st_geometry(black_range_sf), add = TRUE, col = scales::alpha("grey50", 0.9), border = NA)
# RS range
plot(st_geometry(red_range_sf), add = TRUE, col = scales::alpha("#8E063B", 0.7), border = NA)
# Add admix proportions
make.admix.pie.plot(admix.proportions = admix_props, coords = coords, radii = 3.5, layer.colors = c("red","black"),
                    add=TRUE)

# Increase font sizes
par(cex.axis = 1.2,   # Axis tick labels
    cex.lab = 1.5,    # Axis titles
    cex.main = 1.8)   # Main title

# Add map elements
map.axes()

# Legend with larger text
legend("bottomright", 
        legend = c("Red Spruce", "Black Spruce"), 
        fill = c("red", "black"), 
        title = "Ancestry", 
        bty = "n", 
        cex = 1.3,     # Increase legend text size
        title.cex = 1.4) # Increase legend title size
# Add axis titles
title(xlab = "Longitude", ylab = "Latitude", line = 2.5, cex.lab = 1.5)

# Add scale bar
map.scale(x = -75, y = 35, ratio = FALSE, relwidth = 0.15, metric = TRUE, cex = 1.0)

# export pdf - 13 in wide x 7 in height
# export png 1200 x 674
```


```{r echo=T,eval=F}
# Close PDF device
dev.off()
```

#### Admixture inset plot  
```{r echo=T, eval=F}
# Open PDF device
pdf("./figures/paper_figures/Figure2A_inset_July_2025.pdf", 
    width = 19,   # width in inches
    height = 15,   # height in inches
    pointsize = 12)  # font size
```


```{r}
# Draw the base map
map("worldHires","usa", xlim = c(-76.5, -75), ylim = c(40.8, 41.6), col = "gray90", fill = TRUE, border = "gray50")
# map("worldHires","Canada", xlim = c(-76.5, -74.8), ylim = c(40.8, 41.6), col = "gray90", fill = TRUE, border = "gray50", add=TRUE)
map("state", xlim = c(-76.5, -75), ylim = c(40.8, 41.6), col = "gray90", fill = TRUE, border = "gray50", add=TRUE)
# Add lakes
# map("lakes", col = "white", fill = TRUE, add = TRUE)
# Add custom shape files 
## BS range
plot(st_geometry(black_range_sf), add = TRUE, col = scales::alpha("grey50", 0.3), border = NA)
# RS range
plot(st_geometry(red_range_sf), add = TRUE, col = scales::alpha("#8E063B", 0.2), border = NA)

# Add random jitter to coordinates before plotting
jitter_amount <- 0.002  # Adjust this value as needed
coords_jittered <- coords
coords_jittered[,1] <- jitter(coords[,1], amount = jitter_amount)
coords_jittered[,2] <- jitter(coords[,2], amount = jitter_amount)

# Define colors with transparency
layer.colors <- c(adjustcolor("red", alpha.f = 0.5),
                  adjustcolor("black", alpha.f = 0.5))

# Add admix proportions
make.admix.pie.plot(admix.proportions = admix_props, coords = coords, radii = 20, layer.colors = layer.colors,
                    add=TRUE)

# Increase font sizes
par(cex.axis = 1.2,   # Axis tick labels
    cex.lab = 1.5,    # Axis titles
    cex.main = 1.8)   # Main title

# Add map elements
map.axes()

```


```{r echo=T,eval=F}
# Close PDF device
dev.off()
```

### topographic maps  
```{r}
library(tidyterra)  # for the function hypso.colors()
topo_pal <- hypso.colors(n = 10, palette = "dem_poster")



```

```{r}
map_meta <- Meta %>% filter(Experiment == "NSF")
map_meta$Region <- plyr::revalue(map_meta$Region, c("C" = "Core", "M" = "Margin","E" = "Edge"))
garden <- read.csv("../../BlackSpruce/NSF_gardens.csv", header=T)

require(ggrepel)
basemap <- ggplot() +
            geom_spatraster(data = mdt)+
            geom_sf(data = map_area,
                              fill = NA,
                              colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
                      
            scale_fill_hypso_tint_c(palette = "pakistan") + #limits = r_limits,
              
              theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
                      # guides(fill = guide_colorsteps(barwidth = 20,
                      #                                barheight = .5,
                      #                                title.position = "top")) +
                      # labs(fill = "m") +
                      # theme_void() +
                      
            theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
                  plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                  plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
                  plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
                  axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.4),
                  legend.position = "none")+ 
              # black spruce range
              geom_sf(data = bs_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.7, fill = "grey20", alpha = 0.5) +
              # red spruce range
              geom_sf(data = little_range, color = NA,lwd = 1.3, fill="#8E063B",alpha = 0.6)+
              # Add lakes
              geom_sf(data = lakes_transformed, fill = "#A9DEF9") + # , colour = "black", lwd = 0.4
              coord_sf(xlim = c(-85, -59), ylim = c(34, 48), expand = FALSE) #+
              # scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(37,37.5,38, 38.5,39, 39.5,40))

# All labels should be to the right of 3.
x_limits <- c(-70, NA)

basemap + geom_point(data=map_meta,
                     aes(x=Longitude ,y=Latitude, shape = Region), size = 4.5, alpha = 0.4, fill = "#66CDAA", color="black",
                     position = position_jitter(w = 0.05, h = 0)) +
              scale_shape_manual(values = c(21,23,24)) + 
          geom_label_repel(data=garden, color="black", fill = "white", fontface = 'bold', family = 'Arial',
                     aes(x=Longitude ,y=Latitude, label=Garden),
                     size = 4.5,  xlim  = x_limits, label.r = 0, label.padding = 0.45) +
          geom_mark_ellipse(data = map_meta, label.family = "Arial",
                            con.cap = 0, label.position = "above",
                             label.buffer = unit(0.8, "mm"),
                     aes(group = Region, label = Region, x = Longitude, y = Latitude)) +
          geom_point(data=garden,
                     aes(x=Longitude ,y=Latitude), size = 5, fill = "white", color="black", shape=22)
          

# ggsave("./figures/paper_figures/Figure1A.pdf", width = 4000, height = 3500, units = "px")
# ggsave("./figures/paper_figures/Figure1A_editing.pdf", width = 4000, height = 3500, units = "px")

```

# end here

```{r}
simple_basemap <- ggplot() +
                  geom_spatraster(data = mdt)+
                  geom_sf(data = map_area,
                                    fill = NA,
                                    colour = "grey97", lwd = 0.4) +
                            # scale_fill_hypso_tint_c() +
                  # geom_sf_label(aes(label=NAME))+
                  scale_fill_hypso_tint_c(limits = r_limits,palette = "etopo1_hypso") +
                    
                    theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
                            guides(fill = guide_colorsteps(barwidth = 20,
                                                           barheight = .5,
                                                           title.position = "top")) +
                            labs(fill = "m") +
                            # theme_void() +
                            
                  theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
                        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                        plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
                        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
                        axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7))+ 
                      # red spruce range
                    geom_sf(data = little_range, color = "#8E063B",lwd = 1.3, fill=NA)+
                    coord_sf(xlim = c(-90, -59), ylim = c(34, 49), expand = FALSE) #+
                    # scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(37,37.5,38, 38.5,39, 39.5,40))
```

# Old code  

```{r}
usa <- sf::st_as_sf(maps::map("state", fill=TRUE, plot =FALSE))

ggpm <- ggplot(usa) +
          geom_sf(aes(fill = ID == "texas"), colour = "grey20") +
          coord_sf(default_crs = sf::st_crs(4326), ylim = c(10, 50)) + 
          theme(legend.position = "none") +
          scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "red", "FALSE" = "steelblue4"))


ggpm + geom_magnify(aes(from = ID == "texas"),
                    to = c(-125, -98, 10, 30), 
                    shadow = TRUE, linewidth = 1, colour = "orange2",
                    shape = "outline", 
                    aspect = "fixed", 
                    expand = 0) 
```



```{r}
inset_config <- configure_inset(
  shape_sf(states %>% filter(name == "Pennsylvania")),
  scale = 2,
  translation = c(10, -5)
)
library(ggmapinset)
ggplot() +
  # geom_rect_inset(aes(xmin = -90, xmax = -59, ymin = 34, ymax = 49), fill = "white", color = NA) +
  # geom_sf_inset(data = map_area, fill = "grey90", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
  # geom_sf_inset(data = states_sf, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.6) +
  # geom_sf_inset(data = bs_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "grey20", alpha = 0.5) +
  # geom_sf_inset(data = little_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "#8E063B", alpha = 0.5) +
  # geom_sf_inset(data = lakes_transformed, fill = "white", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
  # geom_scatterpie2_inset(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, r = 0.27), 
  #                  data = dataset2 %>% filter(Radius < 0.75), 
  #                  cols = c("RedSpruce", "BlackSpruce"),
  #                  alpha = 0.5,
  #                  legend_name = "Ancestry") +
  # scale_fill_manual(values = c("RedSpruce" = "red", "BlackSpruce" = "black")) + 
  geom_point_inset(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude), 
             data = dataset2 %>% filter(Radius > 0.75),
             shape = 23, size = 5, alpha = 0.8, 
             fill="orange", color="black") + 
  geom_inset_frame() +
  coord_sf_inset(inset_config, expand = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect("white", colour = NA),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.2),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.0),
        axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.4))

inset_cfg <- configure_inset(
  shape_circle(
    centre =  sf::st_centroid(states[states$name == "Pennsylvania",]),
    radius = 50
  ),
  scale = 2,
  units = "mi",
  translation = c(42, -75)
)


int_map + 
  coord_sf_inset(inset_cfg) + coord_sf(xlim = c(-90, -59), ylim = c(34, 48), expand = FALSE)

```



```{r}
# version 2
intmap_v2 <- ggplot() +
             # Add bg
            geom_rect(aes(xmin = -90, xmax = -59, ymin = 34, ymax = 49), fill = "white", color = NA) +
            # base map
            geom_sf(data = map_area, fill = "grey90", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
            geom_sf(data = bs_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "grey20", alpha = 0.5) +
            geom_sf(data = little_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "#8E063B", alpha = 0.5) +
            geom_sf(data = states, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.6) +  # Add state lines
            # lakes
            geom_sf(data = lakes_transformed, fill = "white", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
             
            geom_point(data = dataset2 %>% filter(BlackSpruce < 0.75),
                       aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, fill = BlackSpruce), 
                       size = 8, shape = 21, position = position_jitter()) + 
            scale_fill_gradient("BlackSpruce", low = "white", high = "#251714FF") +
            geom_point(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude), 
                       data = dataset2 %>% filter(Radius > 0.75),
                       shape = 23, size = 5, alpha = 0.8, 
                       fill="orange",color="black") + 
            # geom_label_repel(data=dataset2 %>% filter(Radius > 0.75),
            #                  aes(label=Family,x = Longitude, y = Latitude), size = 10) +
            coord_sf(xlim = c(-90, -59), ylim = c(34, 48), expand = FALSE) +
            theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
            theme(plot.background = element_rect("white", colour = NA),
                  plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                  plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.2),
                  plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.0),
                  axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.4))

intmap_v2 +
```


```{r}
library(ggmapinset)

# Define the inset configuration for Pennsylvania
pa_inset <- configure_inset(
  shape_sf(map_area %>% filter(NAME == "Pennsylvania")),
  scale = 2,
  translation = c(10, -5)
)

ggplot() +
  # Add bg
  geom_rect_inset(aes(xmin = -90, xmax = -59, ymin = 34, ymax = 49), fill = "white", color = NA) +
  # base map
  geom_sf_inset(data = map_area, fill = "grey90", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
  geom_sf_inset(data = bs_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "grey20", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_sf_inset(data = little_range_sf, colour = "black", lwd = 0.4, fill = "#8E063B", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_sf_inset(data = states, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.6) +  # Add state lines
  # lakes
  geom_sf_inset(data = lakes_transformed, fill = "white", colour = "black", lwd = 0.4) +
  
  geom_scatterpie2_inset(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, r = 0.27), 
                   data = dataset2 %>% filter(Radius < 0.75), 
                   cols = c("RedSpruce", "BlackSpruce"),
                   alpha = 0.5,
                   legend_name = "Ancestry") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("RedSpruce" = "red", "BlackSpruce" = "black")) + 
  
  geom_point_inset(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude), 
             data = dataset2 %>% filter(Radius > 0.75),
             shape = 23, 
             size = 5, alpha = 0.8, fill="orange", color="black") + 
  
  geom_inset_frame() +
  coord_sf_inset(pa_inset, expand = FALSE) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect("white", colour = NA),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.2),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.0),
        axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.4))

```


```{r}
basemap2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(data = mdt)+
  geom_sf(data = map_area,
                    fill = NA,
                    colour = "grey97", lwd = 0.4) +
            # scale_fill_hypso_tint_c() +
  # geom_sf_label(aes(label=NAME))+
  # scale_fill_hypso_tint_c(limits = r_limits,palette = "etopo1_hypso") +
    
    theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
            # guides(fill = guide_colorsteps(barwidth = 20,
            #                                barheight = .5,
            #                                title.position = "top")) +
            # labs(fill = "m") +
            # theme_void() +
            
  theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
        axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7))+ 
      # red spruce range
    geom_sf(data = little_range, color = "#8E063B",lwd = 1.3, fill=NA)+
    coord_sf(xlim = c(-90, -59), ylim = c(34, 49), expand = FALSE)
   
basemap + geom_point(data=dataset[dataset$q2<0.75,], aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude, color=q2), 
                           alpha = 1, size = 5, position="jitter")

dataset2 <- dataset[,c("Family","q1","q2","Longitude","Latitude")]
dataset2$Family <- as.factor(dataset2$Family)
dataset2$q1 <- round(dataset2$q1,3)
dataset2$q2 <- round(dataset2$q2,3)
colnames(dataset2) <- c("Family","A","B","Longitude","Latitude")

p + geom_scatterpie(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, group =Family), 
                  data = dataset2, 
                  cols = c("A","B"),
                  alpha = 0.8) +
   scale_fill_discrete() +
    coord_sf(xlim = c(-90, -59), ylim = c(34, 49), expand = FALSE)

require(scatterpie)
basemap + 
  geom_scatterpie(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, group = Family), 
                  data = dataset2, 
                  cols = "letters", color=NA,
                  alpha = 0.8) 

# dataset3 <- dataset
# dataset3$Family <- as.factor(dataset3$Family)
# dataset3$q1 <- round(dataset3$q1,3)
# dataset3$q2 <- round(dataset3$q2,3)
# dataset3$q1 <- as.factor(dataset3$q1)
# dataset3$q2 <- as.factor(dataset3$q2)

ggplot() + geom_scatterpie(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, group = Family), 
                  data = dataset2, 
                  cols = c("q1", "q2"), 
                  alpha = 0.8) + 

world <- map_data('world')
set.seed(123)
long <- rnorm(50, sd=100)
lat <- rnorm(50, sd=50)
d <- data.frame(long=long, lat=lat)
d <- with(d, d[abs(long) < 150 & abs(lat) < 70,])
n <- nrow(d)
d$region <- factor(1:n)
d$A <- abs(rnorm(n, sd=1))
d$B <- abs(rnorm(n, sd=2))
d$C <- abs(rnorm(n, sd=3))
d$D <- abs(rnorm(n, sd=4))
d$radius <- 6 * abs(rnorm(n))
p <- ggplot(world, aes(long, lat)) +
    geom_map(map=world, aes(map_id=region), fill=NA, color="black") +
    coord_quickmap()
p + geom_scatterpie(aes(x=long, y=lat, group=region, r=radius),
                    data=d, cols=LETTERS[1:4], color=NA, alpha=.8)



p + geom_scatterpie(aes(x=long, y=lat, group=region, r=radius),
                    data=d, cols=c("A","B","C","D"), color=NA, alpha=.8)

basemap +
  # geom_spatraster(data = mdt)+
  # geom_sf(data = map_area,
  #                   fill = NA,
  #                   colour = "grey97", lwd = 0.4) +
  geom_scatterpie(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, group = Family), 
                  data = dataset3, 
                  cols = c("q1", "q2"),
                  alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_discrete()


basemap + geom_scatterpie(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude), 
                  data = pie_data, long_format = T,
                  cols = c("Species","Ancestry"),
                  alpha = 0.8)
    

# dataset2 <- dataset
# dataset2$q1 <- as.factor(dataset2$q1)
# dataset2$q2 <- as.factor(dataset2$q2)



ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = as.data.frame(elevation, xy = TRUE), aes(x = x, y = y, fill = layer)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "inferno", name = "Elevation") +
  geom_sf(data = world %>% filter(continent == "North America"), fill = NA, color = "black") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-100, -60), ylim = c(35, 55)) +
  geom_scatterpie(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, group = Family), 
                  data = dataset, 
                  cols = c("q1", "q2"),
                  alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90"),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  labs(title = "Eastern North America Ancestry Map with Elevation")


############### start here



ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world %>% filter(continent == "North America"), fill = "white", color = "white") +
  geom_sf(data = ne_coastline(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf"), color = "blue") +
  # geom_sf(data = ne_lakes(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf"), fill = "blue", color = "blue") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-100, -60), ylim = c(35, 55)) +
  geom_scatterpie(aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, group = Family), 
                  data = dataset, 
                  cols = c("ancestry_score1", "ancestry_score2", "ancestry_score3"),
                  alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Eastern North America Ancestry Map")
```


```{r}
# source: https://wvgis.wvu.edu/data/dataset.php?ID=455
# load data WV range map
# WV_area <- st_read('./redSpruceCoverWV_WVDNR_utm83_2013_shp/redSpruceCoverWV_WVDNR_utm83_2013.shp')
# plot(WV_area)
# ggplot() + geom_sf(WV_area) +
#   theme_minimal()
```


# oct 2023 - current working code
```{r}
# data
info_inds <- read.table("../Info_samples_revised.txt", header=T)
info_pops <- info_inds[!duplicated(info_inds$Site),-c(1,3,9,10)]
info_pops$Region <- factor(info_pops$Region, levels = c("C","M","E"),
                                             labels = c("Core","Margin","Edge"))
info_pops$Longitude <- -(info_pops$Longitude)

# sources
Source_maryland <- info_pops %>% filter(info_pops$Region=="Edge" & !info_pops$State%in%c("NC","TN","VA") &
                                          !info_pops$Site%in%c("XCV","HR"))
Source_west_virginia <- info_pops %>% filter(info_pops$Region=="Edge" & info_pops$State=="WV" &
                                               !info_pops$Site%in%c("XCV","HR"))

Source_virginia <- info_pops %>% filter(info_pops$Region=="Edge"  & (info_pops$State=="WV" & 
                                        !info_pops$Site%in%c("XCV") | info_pops$Site%in%c("GMF","CR","DG","RP")))
Source_maryland$Group <- "Unselected source"
Source_maryland[Source_maryland$Site %in% c("XCS","XDS","XPK"),"Group"] <- "Selected source"

Source_west_virginia$Group <- "Unselected source"
Source_west_virginia[Source_west_virginia$Site %in% c("XCS","XDS","XPK","XSK"),"Group"] <- "Selected source"

Source_virginia$Group <- "Unselected source"
Source_virginia[Source_virginia$Site %in% c("BFA","KOS","XDS","XPK"),"Group"] <- "Selected source"


# sites
site_md1 <- c("Finzel swamp","MD","Edge",39.703,-78.940,NA, "Restoration site")
site_md2 <- c("Cranesville swamp","MD","Edge",39.550,-79.479,NA, "Restoration site")
site_wv <- c("Spruce Mountain GMA","WV","Edge",38.691, -79.599,NA, "Restoration site")
site_va <- c("Clinch Mountain WMA","VA","Edge",36.925, -81.879,NA, "Restoration site")

# merge
Source_maryland <- as.data.frame(rbind(Source_maryland,site_md1,site_md2))
Source_maryland <- type.convert(Source_maryland, as.is = TRUE)
Source_maryland$Group <- factor(Source_maryland$Group, levels = c("Restoration site","Selected source","Unselected source"))

Source_west_virginia <- as.data.frame(rbind(Source_west_virginia,site_wv))
Source_west_virginia <- type.convert(Source_west_virginia, as.is = TRUE)
Source_west_virginia$Group <- factor(Source_west_virginia$Group, levels = c("Restoration site","Selected source","Unselected source"))

Source_virginia <- as.data.frame(rbind(Source_virginia,site_va))
Source_virginia <- type.convert(Source_virginia, as.is = TRUE)
Source_virginia$Group <- factor(Source_virginia$Group, levels = c("Restoration site","Selected source","Unselected source"))




```


```{r}
# testing 
# read in shapefile for map
n_america <- read_sf("../../Maps/geoBoundariesCGAZ_ADM0/geoBoundariesCGAZ_ADM0.shp")
plot(n_america, max.plot=1)

# project polygons into new CRS (NAD83)
n_america_proj <- st_transform(n_america, crs = st_crs(4269))


# Get US states and water bodies
states <- st_as_sf(maps::map("world", plot = FALSE, fill = TRUE))

states_east <- states %>% filter(ID %in% c("alabama", "connecticut", "delaware", "florida", "georgia", "illinois", "indiana", "kentucky", "maine", "maryland", "massachusetts", "michigan", "new hampshire", "new jersey", "new york", "north carolina", "ohio", "pennsylvania", "rhode island", "south carolina", "tennessee", "vermont", "virginia", "west virginia"))

water <- ne_download(scale = "medium", type = "lakes", category = "physical", returnclass = "sf")

plot(states)
plot(states_east)
```


```{r}
# source: https://dominicroye.github.io/en/2022/hillshade-effects/

us_states <- states()
map_area <- us_states[us_states$NAME %in%  c("West Virginia","Virginia","Maryland","Pennsylvania","Tennessee","North Carolina"), ]
plot(map_area, max.plot=1)
plot(us_states, max.plot=1)

# spruce range
little_range<-st_read('../../../datashare/Spruce/SpatialData/rangemaps/spruce/picerube/picerube.shp')

bbox = st_bbox(map_area)
# borders = little_range %>% st_crop(bbox + c(-2, -2, 2, 2))


# Use elevatr to get elevation data
elev <- elevatr::get_elev_raster(
  locations = map_area$geometry, z = 10) # , clip = 'locations'

# saveRDS(elev,"./elevatr/elev_PA_MD_WV_VA_TN_NC_z11")

plot(elev)

# convert to terra and mask area of interest
mdt <- rast(elev) %>% 
         mask(vect(map_area))

# load this for future analysis
# saveRDS(mdt,"./elevatr/elev_PA_MD_WV_VA_TN_NC_z11_masked")

names(mdt) <- "alt"

mdt <- mdt %>%
  mutate(alt = pmax(0, alt))

# load this for future analysis
# saveRDS(mdt,"./elevatr/elev_PA_MD_WV_VA_TN_NC_z11_masked")

plot(mdt)

# reproject
# mdt <- project(mdt, crs(map_area))
```


```{r eval =F, echo=F}
# reproject vect
# suiz <- st_transform(suiz, st_crs(suiz_lakes))

# convert the raster into a data.frame of xyz
mdtdf <- as.data.frame(mdt, xy = TRUE)
names(mdtdf)[3] <- "alt"

# mdtdf <- mdtdf %>%
#   mutate(alt = pmax(0, alt))

```


```{r}
# Hypso gradient
grad_hypso <- hypso.colors2(10, "dem_poster")


# Try some options, but we need to be aware of the values of our raster

r_limits <- minmax(mdt) %>% as.vector()

# Rounded to lower and upper 500
r_limits <- c(floor(r_limits[1] / 500), ceiling(r_limits[2] / 500)) * 500

# And making min value to 0.
r_limits <- pmax(r_limits, 0)


# Now lets have some fun with scales from tidyterra
```


```{r}
elevt_test <- ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(data = mdt)

# Create a helper function

plot_pal_test <- function(pal) {
  elevt_test +
    scale_fill_hypso_tint_c(
      limits = r_limits,
      palette = pal
    ) +
    ggtitle(pal) +
    theme_minimal()
}

plot_pal_test("etopo1_hypso")
plot_pal_test("dem_poster")
plot_pal_test("spain") # chosen + 
plot_pal_test("pakistan")
plot_pal_test("utah_1")
plot_pal_test("wiki-2.0_hypso")
```

```{r}
# load font
myload_fonts <- function(fontname, family,
                         fontdir = tempdir()) {
  fontname_url <- utils::URLencode(fontname)
  fontzip <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip")
  download.file(paste0("https://fonts.google.com/download?family=", fontname_url),
    fontzip,
    quiet = TRUE,
    mode = "wb"
  )
  unzip(fontzip,
    exdir = fontdir,
    junkpaths = TRUE
  )

  # Load fonts
  paths <- list(
    regular = "Regular.ttf",
    bold = "Bold.ttf",
    italic = "Italic.ttf",
    bolditalic = "BoldItalic.ttf"
  )


  namefile <- gsub(" ", "", fontname)
  paths_end <- file.path(
    fontdir,
    paste(namefile, paths, sep = "-")
  )


  names(paths_end) <- names(paths)

  sysfonts::font_add(family,
    regular = paths_end["regular"],
    bold = paths_end["bold"],
    italic = paths_end["italic"],
    bolditalic = paths_end["bolditalic"]
  )

  return(invisible())
}
```


```{r}
# Theming
myload_fonts("Noto Serif", "notoserif", "~/R/googlefonts")
showtext::showtext_auto()

# Adjust text size
base_text_size <- 30

require(ggrepel)

```

# base map
```{r}
basemap <- ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(data = mdt)+
  geom_sf(data = map_area,
                    fill = NA,
                    colour = "grey97", lwd = 1.2) +
            # scale_fill_hypso_tint_c() +
  # geom_sf_label(aes(label=NAME))+
  scale_fill_hypso_tint_c(limits = r_limits,palette = "spain") +
    
    theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
            guides(fill = guide_colorsteps(barwidth = 20,
                                           barheight = .5,
                                           title.position = "right")) +
            labs(fill = "m") +
            # theme_void() +
            
  theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
        axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7))+ 
  
    # red spruce range
    geom_sf(data = little_range, color = "#8E063B",lwd = 1.3, fill=NA)+
    coord_sf(xlim = c(-83, -78.30), ylim = c(36.5, 40), expand = FALSE) + 
    scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(37,37.5,38, 38.5,39, 39.5,40)) 

basemap_long <- ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(data = mdt)+
  geom_sf(data = map_area,
                    fill = NA,
                    colour = "grey97", lwd = 1.2) +
            # scale_fill_hypso_tint_c() +
  # geom_sf_label(aes(label=NAME))+
  scale_fill_hypso_tint_c(limits = r_limits,palette = "spain") +
    
    theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
            guides(fill = guide_colorsteps(barwidth = 20,
                                           barheight = .5,
                                           title.position = "right")) +
            labs(fill = "m") +
            # theme_void() +
            
  theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
                plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
                plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
                axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7),
                legend.position = "bottom",
                legend.title = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.key = element_rect("grey50"),
                legend.spacing.x = unit(0, "pt")
              ) +
                # Change guide
              guides(fill = guide_legend(
                title = "   m.",
                direction = "horizontal",
                nrow = 1,
                keywidth = 5,
                keyheight = 0.5,
                label.position = "bottom",
                title.position = "right",
                override.aes = list(alpha = 1)
              ))+
  
    # red spruce range
    geom_sf(data = little_range, color = "#8E063B",lwd = 1.3, fill=NA)+
    coord_sf(xlim = c(-83, -78.30), ylim = c(35.5, 40), expand = FALSE) + 
    scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(36,36.5,37,37.5,38, 38.5,39, 39.5,40)) 
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(data = mdt)+
  geom_sf(data = map_area,
                    fill = NA,
                    colour = "grey97", lwd = 1.2) +
            # scale_fill_hypso_tint_c() +
  # geom_sf_label(aes(label=NAME))+
  scale_fill_hypso_tint_c(limits = r_limits,palette = "spain") +
    
    theme_minimal() +
            guides(fill = guide_colorsteps(barwidth = 20,
                                           barheight = .5,
                                           title.position = "bottom")) +
            labs(fill = "m") + 
    scale_y_continuous(position="right",sec.axis = sec_axis(~., labels = NULL))+
            # theme_void() +
            
  theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
                plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
                plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
                axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7),
                legend.position = "bottom",
                legend.title = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.key = element_rect("grey50"),
                legend.spacing.x = unit(0, "pt"),
        axis.text.y.right = element_text(margin = unit(c(5, 5, 5, 10), "mm"))
              ) +
                # Change guide
              guides(fill = guide_legend(
                title = "   m.",
                direction = "horizontal",
                nrow = 1,
                keywidth = 5,
                keyheight = 0.5,
                label.position = "bottom",
                title.position = "right",
                override.aes = list(alpha = 1)
              ))+
  
    # red spruce range
    geom_sf(data = little_range, color = "#8E063B",lwd = 1.3, fill=NA)+
    coord_sf(xlim = c(-83, -78.30), ylim = c(35, 40), expand = FALSE) 


ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(data = mdt)+
  # geom_sf(data = map_area,
                    # fill = NA,
                    # colour = "grey97", lwd = 1.2)+
            # scale_fill_hypso_tint_c() +
  # geom_sf_label(aes(label=NAME))+
   scale_x_continuous(position = "top")

# breaks=c(35.5,36,36.5,37,37.5,38, 38.5,39, 39.5,40),
```



```{r}
basemap_long+guides(fill="none")

legend_scale <- basemap_long + theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
                plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
                plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
                axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7),
                legend.position = "bottom",
                legend.title = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.key = element_rect("grey50"),
                legend.spacing.x = unit(0, "pt")
              ) +
                # Change guide
              guides(fill = guide_legend(
                title = "   m.",
                direction = "horizontal",
                nrow = 1,
                keywidth = 5,
                keyheight = 0.5,
                label.position = "bottom",
                title.position = "right",
                override.aes = list(alpha = 1)
              ))

legend_groups <- basemap_long + theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
                plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
                plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
                axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7),
                axis.title=element_text(size=base_text_size* 0.7),
                legend.position = "right",
                legend.title = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.key = element_rect("grey97"),
                legend.spacing.x = unit(4, "pt")
              ) +
              geom_point(data=Source_maryland, aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude,color=Group,shape=Group), 
                           alpha = 1, size = 6, position="jitter")+ 
              scale_color_manual(name = "Legend",
                                 labels = c("Restoration site", "Selected source", "Unselected source"),
                                 values = c("#D8152F","#264DFF","black")) + 
              scale_shape_manual(name = "Legend",
                                 labels = c("Restoration site", "Selected source", "Unselected source"),
                                 values = c(17,19,19))+
              guides(fill="none")
```

```{r}
# inset map
require(maps)
usa <- map_data('state')

inset <- ggplot(data=usa, aes(x=long, y=lat, group=group)) + 
          geom_polygon(color = "grey50",fill="white") + 
          # guides(fill=FALSE) + 
          # theme(axis.title.x=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_blank(), axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
          #       axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.y=element_blank(), axis.ticks.y=element_blank()) + 
          # ggtitle('U.S. Map with States') + 
          # theme_minimal(base_family = "notoserif") +
          # coord_fixed(1.3) + 
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83, -68), ylim=c(45,25),  expand = FALSE)+
          theme(
            plot.background = element_rect("grey90", colour = "black"),
            panel.border=element_rect(color="black"),
            
                        # plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                        # plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
                        # plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
                        axis.text = element_blank(),
                        # 
                        axis.title.x=element_blank(), 
                        axis.text.x=element_blank(), 
                        axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
                        axis.title.y=element_blank(), 
                        axis.text.y=element_blank(), 
                        axis.ticks.y=element_blank()
                      ) + labs(y="",x="")+
  theme_map()+
          geom_rect(aes(xmin = -78.30, xmax = -83, ymin = 35, ymax = 40), 
                    color = "blue", inherit.aes = FALSE, fill=NA, lwd=2)
inset

library("rnaturalearth")
library("rnaturalearthdata")
require(sf)
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")
library("maps")
states <- st_as_sf(map("state", plot = FALSE, fill = TRUE))
states <- cbind(states, st_coordinates(st_centroid(states)))
ggplot(data = world) +
    geom_sf() +
    geom_sf(data = states, fill = NA) + 
    geom_label(data = states, aes(X, Y, label = ID), size = 5, fontface = "bold", 
        nudge_y = states$nudge_y) +
    coord_sf(xlim = c(-88, -78), ylim = c(24.5, 33), expand = FALSE)

# xlim = c(-83, -78.30), ylim = c(35.5, 40)


  

maps::map("state", interior=FALSE)
maps::map("state", boundary=FALSE, col="gray", add=TRUE)

# require(mapdata)


```


```{r}
require(ggspatial)
require(tmap)
MD_plot <- basemap_long + theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
                plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
                plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
                axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7),
                axis.title=element_text(size=base_text_size* 0.7),
                legend.position = "right",
                legend.title = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.key = element_rect("grey97"),
                legend.spacing.x = unit(0, "pt")) +
              
              geom_point(data=Source_maryland, aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude,color=Group,shape=Group), 
                           alpha = 1, size = 5, position="jitter")+ 
              scale_color_manual(name = "Legend",
                                 labels = c("Restoration site", "Selected source", "Unselected source"),
                                 values = c("#D8152F","#264DFF","black")) + 
              scale_shape_manual(name = "Legend",
                                 labels = c("Restoration site", "Selected source", "Unselected source"),
                                 values = c(17,19,19))+
                # selected sources
                # geom_point(data=Source_maryland %>% filter(Source_maryland$Site %in%c("XCS","XDS","XPK","Finzel swamp","Cranesville swamp")),
                #                    aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude),
                #                    size = 4, alpha = 1, color="darkred",position="jitter")+
              geom_label_repel(data = Source_maryland %>% filter(Source_maryland$Site %in%c("XCS","XDS","XPK","Finzel swamp","Cranesville swamp")),
                              aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude, label=Site),
                              # nudge_x       = 40 ,
                              size          = 5,
                              box.padding   = 0.5,
                              point.padding = 0.5,
                              # force         = 100,
                              # segment.size  = 0.8,
                              segment.color = "black",
                              # direction     = "x",
                              # show.legend = FALSE,
                              max.overlaps = 10) +
              labs(y="Latitude", x="Longitude",
                   title = "Maryland") + 
              guides(fill="none",color="none",shape="none") +
  
  annotation_north_arrow(location = "tl", style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering)


```



```{r}
WV_plot <- basemap_long + theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
                plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
                plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
                axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7),
                axis.title=element_text(size=base_text_size* 0.7),
                axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
                legend.position = "right",
                legend.title = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.key = element_rect("grey97"),
                legend.spacing.x = unit(0, "pt")) +
              
              geom_point(data=Source_west_virginia, aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude,color=Group,shape=Group), 
                           alpha = 1, size = 5, position="jitter")+ 
              scale_color_manual(name = "Legend",
                                 labels = c("Restoration site", "Selected source", "Unselected source"),
                                 values = c("#D8152F","#264DFF","black")) + 
              scale_shape_manual(name = "Legend",
                                 labels = c("Restoration site", "Selected source", "Unselected source"),
                                 values = c(17,19,19))+
                
              geom_label_repel(data = Source_west_virginia %>% filter(Source_west_virginia$Site %in%c("XCS","XDS","XPK","XSK","Spruce Mountain GMA")),
                              aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude, label=Site),
                              size          = 5,
                              box.padding   = 0.5,
                              point.padding = 0.5,
                              segment.color = "black",
                              max.overlaps = 10) +
  labs(y="", x="Longitude",
                   title = "West Virginia") + guides(fill="none",color="none",shape="none")


WV_plot + annotation_custom(ggplotGrob(inset), xmin = -80, xmax = -78.5, 
                       ymin = 35.5, ymax = 37)
```

```{r}
VA_plot <- basemap_long + theme(plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
                plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
                plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
                plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
                axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7),
                axis.title=element_text(size=base_text_size* 0.7),
                axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
                legend.position = c(0.75,0.10),
                legend.title = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
                legend.key = element_rect("grey97"),
                legend.spacing.x = unit(0, "pt")) +
              
              
              geom_point(data=Source_virginia, aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude,color=Group,shape=Group), 
                           alpha = 1, size = 5, position="jitter")+ 
              scale_color_manual(name = "Legend",
                                 labels = c("Restoration site", "Selected source", "Unselected source"),
                                 values = c("#D8152F","#264DFF","black")) + 
              scale_shape_manual(name = "Legend",
                                 labels = c("Restoration site", "Selected source", "Unselected source"),
                                 values = c(17,19,19))+
                
              geom_label_repel(data = Source_virginia %>% filter(Source_virginia$Site %in%c("BFA","KOS","XDS","XPK","Clinch Mountain WMA")),
                              aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude, label=Site),
                              size          = 5,
                              box.padding   = 0.5,
                              point.padding = 0.5,
                              segment.color = "black",
                              max.overlaps = 10) +
              labs(y="", x="Longitude",
                   title = "Virginia") + guides(fill="none")
```


```{r}
require(cowplot)
legend_bottom <- get_legend(legend_scale + theme(legend.position="bottom"))
legend_right <- get_legend(legend_groups+ theme(legend.position="right"))

 cowplot::plot_grid(MD_plot,WV_plot,VA_plot,+ theme(legend.position="none"), legend_ba, ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(1,.15))


map_grid <- cowplot::plot_grid( MD_plot + theme(legend.position="none"),
           WV_plot + theme(legend.position="none"),
           VA_plot + theme(legend.position="none"),
           align = 'vh',
           # labels = c("A", "B", "C", "D"),
           hjust = -1,
           nrow = 1
           )

```

```{r}
require(ggpubr)
ggarrange(MD_plot,WV_plot,VA_plot, ncol = 3,widths = c(1.1,1,1))

grd1 <- ggarrange(MD_plot,WV_plot,VA_plot, ncol = 3,  widths = c(1.1,1,1), heights=c(1,1,1))

require(cowplot)
legend_bottom <- get_legend(legend_scale + theme(legend.position="bottom"))
legend_right <- get_legend(legend_groups+ theme(legend.position="right"))
leg <-cowplot::plot_grid(legend_bottom, legend_right, ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(1,.5))


# dim 2500w 1067h jpg, 10h 24w pdf
grd2 <- ggarrange(grd1, legend_bottom, nrow = 2,  heights = c(0.95,0.05))
grd2
# grd3 <- 
  # ggarrange(grd2,legend_right, ncol = 2,  widths = c(0.95,0.05))

# cowplot::plot_grid(grd1, leg, nrow = 2)






```



```{r}
# WV

p2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(data = mdt)+
  geom_sf(data = map_area,
                    fill = NA,
                    colour = "white", size=1000) +
            # scale_fill_hypso_tint_c() +
  scale_fill_hypso_tint_c(limits = r_limits,palette = "spain") +
  
    
   
            guides(fill = guide_colorsteps(barwidth = 20,
                                           barheight = .5,
                                           title.position = "right")) +
            labs(fill = "m") +
            theme_void() +
            theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  
    # red spruce range
    geom_sf(data = little_range, color = "darkred",size=1000, fill=NA)+
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83, -78.30), ylim = c(36.5, 40), expand = FALSE) + 
              labs(y="", x="Longitude") + 
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect("grey97", colour = NA),
    plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.5),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_text_size * 1.4),
    axis.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.7),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
    legend.text = element_text(size = base_text_size * 0.8),
    legend.key = element_rect("grey50"),
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0, "pt"),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  ) +
    # Change guide
  guides(fill = guide_legend(
    title = "   m.",
    direction = "horizontal",
    nrow = 1,
    keywidth = 5,
    keyheight = 0.5,
    label.position = "bottom",
    title.position = "right",
    override.aes = list(alpha = 1)
  )) 

require(ggpubr)
ggarrange(MD_plot,MD_plot,MD_plot, ncol = 3)
```




# hill shading  -- skipped
```{r}
## Create hillshade effect

slope <- terrain(mdt, "slope", unit = "radians")
aspect <- terrain(mdt, "aspect", unit = "radians")
hill <- shade(slope, aspect, 30, 270)

# normalize names
names(hill) <- "shades"

# Hillshading, but we need a palette
pal_greys <- hcl.colors(1000, "Grays")

ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(data = hill) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = pal_greys, na.value = NA)

# Use a vector of colors
index <- hill %>%
  mutate(index_col = rescale(shades, to = c(1, length(pal_greys)))) %>%
  mutate(index_col = round(index_col)) %>%
  pull(index_col)


# Get cols
vector_cols <- pal_greys[index]

# Need to avoid resampling
# and dont use aes

hill_plot <- ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(
    data = hill, fill = vector_cols, maxcell = Inf,
    alpha = 1
  )

hill_plot
```



```{r}
# MD data add
            # all sources
            geom_point(data=Source_maryland, aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude), alpha = 0.60, color = "grey22", size = 4)+ 
            # selected sources
            geom_point(data=Source_maryland %>% filter(Source_maryland$Site %in%c("XCS","XDS","XPK")),
                       aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude), size = 4, alpha = 1, color="darkred")
#+
  
geom_text_repel(data = Source_maryland %>% filter(Source_maryland$Site %in%c("XCS","XDS","XPK")),
                  aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude, label=Site),
                  # nudge_y       = 20 ,
                  size          = 5,
                  box.padding   = 2.5,
                  point.padding = 0.5,
                  force         = 100,
                  segment.size  = 0.8,
                  segment.color = "grey50",
                  direction     = "x",
                  show.legend = FALSE)

# estimate the slope
sl <- terrain(mdt, "slope", unit = "radians")
plot(sl)

# estimate the aspect or orientation
asp <- terrain(mdt, "aspect", unit = "radians")
plot(asp)
```


```{r}
# Convert elevation raster to matrix
elmat <- raster_to_matrix(elev)

# Calculate a color for each point on the surface using the surface normals and
# hemispherical UV mapping.
sunshade <- elmat %>%
  sphere_shade(sunangle = 315, texture = 'bw')

# Convert sunshade to raster
require(recolorize)
dd <- array_to_RasterStack(
  sunshade,
  type = 'stack',
  alpha_mask = TRUE,
  return_alpha = FALSE
)

# Assign dimensions
dd@extent@xmin <- elev@extent@xmin
dd@extent@xmax <- elev@extent@xmax
dd@extent@ymin <- elev@extent@ymin
dd@extent@ymax <- elev@extent@ymax

# Assign projection
crs(dd) <- crs(elev)


cols <- c('#FFF062', 'green4', 'lightblue', '#D26799')
ggplot() +
  # geom_sf(data = lands, aes(fill = OWNER), color = NA) +
  # geom_sf(data = water, color = NA, fill = 'lightblue', size = 0.6, alpha = 1) +
  geom_raster(elev)+
  geom_sf(data = map_area, color = 'black', fill = NA) +
  # geom_sf_text_repel(data = map_area, aes(label = NAME),
  #                    size = 2.8, bg.color = 'white', color = 'black') +
  coord_sf(datum = sf::st_crs(map_area)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = alpha(cols, 0.4))

ggRGB(dd, r = 1, g = 2, b = 3, maxpixels = 5e+08) +
  geom_sf(data = water, color = NA, fill = 'lightblue', size = 0.6, alpha = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = utah_counties, color = 'white', fill = NA) +
  geom_sf_text_repel(data = utah_counties, aes(label = NAME),
                     size = 2.8, bg.color = 'white', color = 'black') +
  coord_sf(datum = sf::st_crs(utah_counties))




require(rayshader)
# Convert elevation raster to matrix
elmat <- raster_to_matrix(dem_terra)

# Calculate a color for each point on the surface using the surface normals and
# hemispherical UV mapping.
sunshade <- elmat %>%
  sphere_shade(sunangle = 315, texture = 'bw')

# Convert sunshade to raster
dd <- array_to_RasterStack(
  sunshade,
  type = 'stack',
  alpha_mask = TRUE,
  return_alpha = FALSE
)

# Assign dimensions
dd@extent@xmin <- dem_terra@extent@xmin
dd@extent@xmax <- elev@extent@xmax
dd@extent@ymin <- elev@extent@ymin
dd@extent@ymax <- elev@extent@ymax

# Assign projection
crs(dd) <- crs(elev)





require(raster)
usa_dem <- getData('alt', country='USA', mask=TRUE)


require(elevatr)

USA <- ne_countries(scale = 110,
                    type = "countries",
                    continent = NULL,
                    country = "United States",
                    geounit = NULL,
                    sovereignty = NULL,
                    returnclass = c("sp", "sf"))
plot(USA)


sub_world <- world[world$name=="United States",]

elevation <- get_elev_raster(sub_world, z = 9, src = "aws")


map <- get_stamenmap( bbox = c(left = 110, bottom = -40, right = 160, top = -10), zoom = 4, maptype = "terrain")


ggmap(map) + 
    theme_void() + 
    # ggtitle(i) + 
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(colour = "orange"), 
      panel.border = element_rect(colour = "grey", fill=NA, size=2))


```


# usmap

```{r}
require(usmap)
require(dplyr)
require(usmapdata)

map <- plot_usmap()

filtered_data <- us_map() %>%
  filter(x >= min_lon, x <= max_lon, y >= min_lat, y <= max_lat)

ggplot(data = filtered_data, aes(x = x, y = y, group = group)) +
  geom_polygon(fill = "lightgrey", color = "white") +
  coord_fixed(xlim = c(min_lon, max_lon), ylim = c(min_lat, max_lat)) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Maryland Map within Bounding Box")

plot_usmap(regions = "states",include = c("MD", "WV", "VA"), labels=T) +
  labs(title = "") +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(color = "black")) #+
  coord_sf(xlim = c(min(bbx[1,]), max(bbx[1,])), 
           ylim = c(min(bbx[2,]), max(bbx[2,])),
           expand = FALSE)
  



plot_usmap(data = countypov, values = "pct_pov_2014", include = c("MD", "WV", "VA"), color = "blue") + 
  scale_fill_continuous(low = "white", high = "blue", name = "Poverty Percentage Estimates", label = scales::comma) + 
  labs(title = "New England Region", subtitle = "Poverty Percentage Estimates for New England Counties in 2014") +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

require(tmap)
require(tmaptools)
require(tigris)

```


# test on thursday
```{r}

# install.packages(c("sf", "raster", "here", "ggplot2", "viridis"))
# remotes::install_github("wmgeolab/rgeoboundaries")
library(rgeoboundaries)
library(sf)
library(raster)
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)
library(dplyr)

library(MODIStsp)
# Downloading the country boundary of Zimbabwe
map_boundary <- geoboundaries("Zimbabwe")
# Defining filepath to save downloaded spatial file
spatial_filepath <- "LandCoverData/zimbabwe.shp"
# Saving downloaded spatial file on to our computer
st_write(map_boundary, paste0(spatial_filepath))

MODIStsp(gui             = FALSE,
         out_folder      = "LandCoverData",
         out_folder_mod  = "LandCoverData",
         selprod         = "LandCover_Type_Yearly_500m (MCD12Q1)",
         bandsel         = "LC1", 
         user            = "anoobvinu" ,
         password        = "Twilight1990#",
         start_date      = "2022.01.01", 
         end_date        = "2022.12.31", 
         verbose         = FALSE,
         spatmeth        = "file",
         spafile         = spatial_filepath,
         out_format      = "GTiff")
# Downloading the boundary of Zimbabwe
map_boundary <- geoboundaries("Zimbabwe")

# Reading in the downloaded landcover raster data
IGBP_raster <- raster(here::here("LandCoverData/zimbabwe/LandCover_Type_Yearly_500m_v6/LC1/MCD12Q1_LC1_2019_001.tif"))

# Transforming data
IGBP_raster <- projectRaster(IGBP_raster, crs = "+proj=longlat +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +no_defs")

# Cropping data
IGBP_raster <- raster::mask(IGBP_raster, as_Spatial(map_boundary))

# Converting the raster object into a dataframe and converting the IGBP classification into a factor
IGBP_df <- as.data.frame(IGBP_raster, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(MCD12Q1_LC1_2019_001 = as.factor(round(MCD12Q1_LC1_2019_001)))
rownames(IGBP_df) <- c()
# Renaming IGBP classification levels
levels(IGBP_df$MCD12Q1_LC1_2019_001) <- c( "Evergreen needleleaf forests",
                                           "Evergreen broadleaf forests",
                                           "Deciduous needleleaf forests",
                                           "Deciduous broadleaf forests",
                                           "Mixed forests",
                                           "Closed shrublands",
                                           "Open shrublands",
                                           "Woody savannas",
                                           "Savannas",
                                           "Grasslands",
                                           "Permanent wetlands",
                                           "Croplands",
                                           "Urban and built-up lands",
                                           "Cropland/natural vegetation mosaics",
                                           "Snow and ice",
                                           "Barren",
                                           "Water bodies")
# Visualising using ggplot2
ggplot() + 
  # geom_raster(data = IGBP_df,
  #             aes(x = x, y = y, fill = MCD12Q1_LC1_2019_001)) +
  geom_sf(data = map_boundary, inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = NA) +
  scale_fill_viridis(name = "Land Cover Type", discrete = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Land Cover classification in Zimbabwe",
       subtitle = "01-01-2019 - 31-12-2019",
       x = "Longitude",
       y = "Latitude") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Downloading the boundary of Zimbabwe
map_boundary <- gb_adm1("USA")
```



```{r}
# us_states <- readOGR("us-boundaries/us_bound_pop.shp")

require(sf)
require(fs)
testSHP <- read_sf("../maps/APPS/cb_2018_us_state_500k.shp")

testSHP %>% ggplot() +geom_sf()


MD_area <- testSHP[testSHP$NAME=="Maryland","STATEFP"]
WV_area <- testSHP[testSHP$NAME=="West Virginia","STATEFP"]
VA_area <- testSHP[testSHP$NAME=="Virginia","STATEFP"]



read_sf('../maps/APPS/cb_2018_us_state_500k.shp') %>%
  filter(NAME %in% c("Maryland", "West Virginia", "Virginia")) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = NAME))
```



# try 200

```{r}
# Define ggplot function
theme_gppr <- function(){ 
    
    theme_void() %+replace%    # replace elements we want to change
    
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(
                   margin = margin(b = 2),
                   size = 14,
                   color = 'black',
                   face = 'bold',
                   hjust = 0),
      
      plot.subtitle = element_text(
                   margin = margin(b = 2),
                   size = 12,
                   color = 'black',
                   face = 'italic',
                   hjust = 0),
      
      plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 8),
      
      legend.position = 'right',
      legend.title = element_text(size = 9),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
      
      plot.margin = unit(c(0.1, 0.0, 0.2, 0.0), "in")
    )
}
```


```{r}
# Load libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tigris)
library(sf)
library(raster)
library(RStoolbox)
library(ggsflabel)
library(rayshader)
library(elevatr)

# Load waterbodies shapefile
water <- sf::st_read('shapes/utah_water.shp', quiet = T)

# Load land ownership shapefile
lands <- sf::st_read('../maps/APPS/cb_2018_us_state_500k.shp', quiet = T)

# Get Utah counties using tigris
utah_counties <- counties(
  state = 'Utah', cb = TRUE,
  resolution = '20m', progress_bar = FALSE)

# Use elevatr to get elevation data
elev <- elevatr::get_elev_raster(
  locations = utah_counties$geometry, z = 7, clip = 'locations'
)
cols <- c('#FFF062', 'green4', 'lightblue', '#D26799')



ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = lands, aes(fill = NAME), color = "black") +
  # geom_sf(data = water, color = NA, fill = 'lightblue', size = 0.6, alpha = 1) +
  # geom_sf(data = utah_counties, color = 'black', fill = NA) +
  # geom_sf_text_repel(data = utah_counties, aes(label = NAME),
  #                    size = 2.8, bg.color = 'white', color = 'black') +
  coord_sf(datum = sf::st_crs(tiff_file)) +
  # scale_fill_manual(values = alpha(cols, 0.4)) +
  labs(title = 'Land Adminstrative Ownership',
       subtitle = 'State of Utah',
       caption = 'Data source: https://gis.utah.gov/data/cadastre/land-ownership',
       fill = 'Owership') +
  theme_gppr()

# Elevation map
ggRGB(dd, r = 1, g = 2, b = 3, maxpixels = 5e+08) +
  geom_sf(data = water, color = NA, fill = 'lightblue', size = 0.6, alpha = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = utah_counties, color = 'white', fill = NA) +
  geom_sf_text_repel(data = utah_counties, aes(label = NAME),
                     size = 2.8, bg.color = 'white', color = 'black') +
  coord_sf(datum = sf::st_crs(utah_counties)) +
  labs(title = 'Digital Elevation Model',
       subtitle = 'State of Utah',
       caption = 'Data source: https://registry.opendata.aws/terrain-tiles',
       fill = 'Owership') +
  theme_gppr()
```
